title: IAM Policy Over-Restriction Blocking Application Deployments
symptoms: CI/CD pipeline failing with AccessDenied errors on S3 artifact upload
ECR image push returning authorization failures
Deployment role showing reduced permission set after recent IAM update
All deployment pipelines affected across all environments
Manual AWS CLI commands with the role also failing
root_cause: IAM policy hardening removed essential deployment permissions (s3:PutObject and ecr:PushImage) from the CI/CD service role without validating against actual permission usage
resolution: Added back the required S3 and ECR permissions to the deployment IAM role, validated all deployment pipelines operational, and implemented IAM policy simulation testing
preventive_actions: Implement IAM policy simulation testing before applying permission changes
Create IAM permission dependency map for CI/CD pipelines
Use AWS Access Analyzer to validate policy changes against actual usage
Implement staged IAM policy rollout with canary testing
Add IAM policy change approval workflow with deployment team review

timeline.event: IAM policy update applied to deployment role
First deployment pipeline fails with AccessDenied
Second deployment attempt fails, pattern noticed
Tom Williams reports all pipelines failing
David Kim investigates, identifies recent IAM policy change
Removed permissions confirmed as root cause
Required permissions added back to deployment role

embedding_summary: IAM policy hardening accidentally removed S3 and ECR permissions from CI/CD deployment role, blocking all production deployments for 1.5 hours. Three critical security hotfixes delayed. Resolved by restoring required permissions and implementing IAM policy simulation testing.
